frontierState:
June 2026 SOTA for black-box (API-teacher, no-logits) distillation, per research/strategy-2026/raw-tech-frontier/tf1-distillation-sota.json:
1. GAD (Generative Adversarial Distillation), arXiv:2511.10643 (Ye et al, 2025-11-13, Microsoft) — #1 for black-box. Student=generator vs a co-evolving discriminator that acts as an on-policy reward model over teacher-vs-student TEXT. Closes most of the SeqKD gap (3-5+ pts); Qwen2.5-14B+GAD reaches GPT-5-Chat parity on LMSYS. Open-source artifacts (ytianzhu.github.io/Generative-Adversarial-Distillation).
2. GKD / on-policy KD, arXiv:2306.13649 (Agarwal et al, ICLR2024); MiniLLM reverse-KL arXiv:2306.08543 (Gu et al).
3. Best-of-N / rejection-sampling distill — BOND arXiv:2407.14622, BRTS arXiv:2605.09725 (2026); +2-4 pts on reasoning/alignment, combines with on-policy.
4. ROPD (Rubric-based On-Policy Distillation) arXiv:2605.07396 — induce per-prompt rubric from teacher TEXT, score student rollouts, GRPO; black-box, ~10x sample efficiency.
5. GrayKD multi-rationale injection (AAAI 2026); CoT/reasoning-path distillation w/ self-consistency filtering.
Startup recommendation: GAD + GKD/BoN give the biggest jump capturing real API traffic; all logit-free.
alreadyFrontier: ROPD (arXiv:2605.07396), the #4 frontier black-box on-policy method, is implemented as a REAL GPU GRPO loop (apps/trainer/ropd.py:555+) with a deterministic pure-core (rubric_score, grpo_advantages, ropd_step) and CLI dispatch (kolm distill onpolicy --ropd) — this is genuinely at the frontier and logit-free
DistiLLM-2 contrastive SKL/SRKL (arXiv:2503.07067, ICML2025 Oral) implemented as real torch in distill.py:879-921 with correct log-space skew + detached teacher anchor
GKD via trl GKDTrainer with lmbda on-policy schedule (workers/distill/scripts/train_gkd.py) — frontier #2
MiniLLM reverse-KL (arXiv:2306.08543) and JSD objectives are real loss fns (distill.py:833,842), selectable via --objective reverse_kl|jsd
Rejection-sampling distillation exists as a first-class pipeline mode and strategy (frontier #3 family)
GRPO/RLVR with verifiable reward families sharing the K-Score evaluator code path (src/distill-grpo.js) — train-eval-mismatch becomes a hard error
CoT/reasoning-trace distillation (distill_cot.py) with byte-exact think-tag envelope matching the runtime template
On-policy student-sample generation (distill.py:987) addressing exposure bias, the core insight the research stresses over plain SeqKD
Honest no-trainer / no-pool envelopes everywhere (never fakes a run when torch/backend absent) — matches the research's emphasis on real measurable jumps

gaps.gap: GAD (Generative Adversarial Distillation, arXiv:2511.10643) — the single #1-ranked frontier method for black-box API-teacher distillation — is entirely absent. Zero discriminator/adversarial/minimax code in apps/trainer or src (grep for GAD|adversarial|discriminator in apps/trainer returns nothing). This is the headline 2025-11 result that closes most of the SeqKD gap and the research explicitly names it the biggest single quality jump for kolm's exact use case (capturing real API traffic).

The recommender oracle src/distill-strategy.js is stale relative to the engine. planDistillStrategy never recommends ropd, gkd, distillm2, reverse_kl/MiniLLM, or cot — the actual frontier methods kolm ships. It still ranks kd_softmax/kd_top_k (logit-distillation, impossible against API teachers) as top picks for generation/chat (distill-strategy.js:200,202). A customer with only Anthropic/OpenAI traffic is steered to a method that cannot run, instead of ROPD which is built and ideal.

No Best-of-N / BOND (arXiv:2407.14622) target curation. rejection_sampling keeps the single best teacher sample; there is no BoN distribution-matching (Jeffreys divergence) nor BoN-from-student-rollouts curation, which the research lists as a +2-4pt complementary layer over on-policy.

ROPD difficulty-anchor and rubric induction quality depend on a teacher-text JUDGE, but there is no automated GPT-4o/Claude-as-judge eval-delta harness wired to report the numeric SeqKD-vs-ROPD-vs-GAD jump per run. The research stresses validating with judge/human prefs; W808 gate only compares K-Score deltas internally, not head-to-head method bake-offs.

ourState:
The platform has a REAL (non-stub) but FRAGMENTED quantization stack split across three disconnected layers.

(1) ORACLE — src/quantization-oracle.js is a genuine deterministic constraint solver (not a stub): METHOD_CATALOG (lines 9-156) holds 12 methods — fp16, int8, smoothquant, int4(NF4), gptq, awq, hqq, exl2, aqlm, quip, qat(EfficientQAT), kivi_kv. scoreCandidate (260-310) ranks by fit/quality/latency/compression/worker-availability with runtime + memory hard-fails and honest warnings (requires_external_research_repo, not_in_quantize_worker). CRITICAL GAP: grep for nvfp4/mxfp4/fp4/blackwell in this file returns ZERO hits — the oracle has no FP4/NVFP4/MXFP4 method entry and DEVICE_PRESETS (158-167) tops out at h100-80gb with NO Blackwell/B200/sm_100 preset (runtime 'tensorrt'). quality_loss values (e.g. awq 0.025, gptq 0.035, int4 0.05 lines 54/66/72) are HARDCODED heuristics, not measured benchmarks. The oracle's proof[] (366-370) says 'run holdout eval before promoting' but it is advisory string text only — no automated accuracy gate is wired.

(2) EXECUTION — workers/quantize/scripts/quantize.py (1500 lines) is a real heavy-lift worker: int4/int8 (bitsandbytes), gptq (auto-gptq), awq (autoawq), aqlm/quip/qat via $REPO_PATH drivers, exl2/exl3 (exllamav2), hqq, plus W719 mixed-precision DAQ (load_mixed_precision_profile 653), W921 MoE-aware grouping (group_moe_parameters 868, router kept fp16), and W921 --calib-fp4 driver (run_fp4_calibration 558) that calls fp4_calib.py.

(3) FP4 — these exist and are REAL but the oracle never reaches them: fp4_calib.py implements BATQuant-style (arXiv:2603.16590) block-wise learnable clipping (fit_block_clip 157) + per-column diagonal affine transform (fit_block_diag_transform 248) over the true E2M1 grid {0,.5,1,1.5,2,3,4,6}; deterministic, CPU, self-tested (self_test 464, 6 checks). src/export-nvfp4.js drives nvidia-modelopt with a real Blackwell gate (probeBlackwellNative 88 via nvidia-smi compute_cap>=10) and w4a4/w4a8/w8a8 levels. src/fp4-calib-plan.js (isFp4Target/buildFp4CalibPlan) decides when to emit --calib-fp4 flags. NONE of these are referenced by quantization-oracle.js.

(4) BAKEOFF — src/quantize-bakeoff.js spawns the worker per candidate DAQ profile and computes a real Pareto frontier (190-200), but scoreJaccard (210) is misnamed: it is a weight-bits surrogate (avgBits/8 + log coverage), NOT token Jaccard, unless $KOLM_BAKEOFF_SCORE_CMD is set. So accepted profiles are ranked by bit-width proxy, not real accuracy, in the default path.

Tests: tests/wave582-quantization-oracle.test.js (asserts catalog covers the 12 methods — does NOT assert any FP4 method), tests/wave921-fp4-calib-trust-remote.test.js, tests/wave921-moe-quantize.test.js, tests/wave195-quantize-worker.test.js. Oracle is wired into HTTP at src/router.js:2914/2954 and scripts/quantization-oracle.mjs CLI.
frontierState: As of June 2026 (research/strategy-2026/raw-tech-frontier/tf2-quantization-fp4-sota.json): NVFP4 (Blackwell-native FP4: E2M1 elements + E4M3 micro-block scales of 16 + tensor-level FP32 scale) is the leading 4-bit format — PTQ via LLM Compressor or TensorRT Model Optimizer yields <1% degradation vs FP8 on many tasks, ~99% BF16 recovery for 70B+, 97-99% for ~30B, 95-98% for 7-14B; native in vLLM and especially TensorRT-LLM on B200/RTX50. MXFP4 (E2M1 + E8M0 power-of-2 scales, group 32) is slightly behind due to scale precision. BATQuant (arXiv:2603.16590, Mar 17 2026) — block-wise learnable affine transform aligned to MX granularity — recovers up to ~96.4% FP at W4A4KV16. Weight-only baselines: AWQ (arXiv:2306.00978) ~1-1.6pt drop, default production INT4; GPTQ (arXiv:2210.17323) ~1.9pt. SpinQuant (arXiv:2405.16406, learned rotations) ~2.9pt on LLaMA-2-7B W4A4KV4, beats QuaRot. QuIP# (arXiv:2307.13304 / PMLR v235 tseng24a, Hadamard incoherence + E8 lattice) SOTA for <=2-bit. AQLM/HQQ for low-bit/fast calibration. QAT/QAD (LLM-QAT) highest fidelity (<1-2pt at W4A4) but training-cost. Sub-4-bit: 5-20+pt gaps, QuIP#/AQLM/lattice help, llama.cpp GGUF Q2/Q3. Recommended auto-select stance: NVFP4 PTQ + block-granular/BATQuant calibration on Blackwell, model-size-aware fallbacks (AWQ/GPTQ legacy, QAT critical, QuIP#/AQLM sub-4-bit), and ALWAYS run a lightweight task-specific eval (MMLU subset / held-out perplexity) post-quant with a threshold (<2-3% relative drop) before deploy. Single biggest NVFP4 risk: calibration/model-specific sensitivity causing outsized drops on <14B models or long-chain reasoning (2-5%+ absolute) without optimal block scaling/affine transforms, plus silent kernel-immaturity under-delivery — must validate numerically per model.
alreadyFrontier: BATQuant-style FP4 calibration (arXiv:2603.16590) is genuinely implemented in fp4_calib.py: real E2M1 grid, block-wise learnable clipping (fit_block_clip), per-column diagonal affine transform with full-precision-equivalence fold, deterministic + self-tested — this is current-frontier and rare to have in-repo.

NVFP4 export via nvidia-modelopt with a correct Blackwell-native gate (probeBlackwellNative reads compute_cap>=10 from nvidia-smi and blocks/ warns on Hopper/Ada emulation) — matches the research's explicit hardware-maturity caveat.
Broad SOTA method coverage in both the oracle catalog and the real worker: AWQ, GPTQ, QuIP#, AQLM, HQQ, EXL2/3, EfficientQAT(QAT), KIVI KV-cache — the full menu the research ranks.
MoE-aware quantization (group_moe_parameters) keeps the router/gate at fp16 and quantizes experts aggressively — correct and not mentioned as standard even at frontier.

Honest execution-status taxonomy (worker / external_toolchain / worker_external_repo / runtime_policy) so the oracle never recommends an unrunnable method without a warning — directly serves the 'avoid silent degradation' goal.
fp4-calib-plan.js correctly gates calibration to FP4-weight targets (w4a4/w4a8 in, w8a8 out) matching the research's point that FP4 calibration is pointless for FP8/INT targets.

gaps.gap: The oracle (quantization-oracle.js METHOD_CATALOG) has NO NVFP4 or MXFP4 method entry and NO Blackwell device preset, so the platform's auto-selector NEVER recommends the research's #1 4-bit format. A user on a B200 asking the oracle gets gptq/awq/int4, not nvfp4 — the FP4 export/calibration code exists but is unreachable from the decision layer.

No automated post-quantization accuracy gate. The oracle's 'run holdout eval before promoting' is advisory text (line 369) and the bakeoff's default scoreJaccard is a bit-width surrogate, not real accuracy. The research names 'silent accuracy degradation' as THE risk and prescribes a numeric per-model eval threshold (<2-3% relative drop) before deploy — we have no enforced gate.

scoreJaccard in quantize-bakeoff.js is misnamed and does not compute token Jaccard against eval_set outputs in the default path — it returns avgBits/8 + log(coverage). Pareto 'accepted' profiles are therefore ranked by a proxy that correlates with size, not measured quality, unless an external scorer env var is set.

SpinQuant (learned rotations, arXiv:2405.16406) — a top W4A4KV4 method in the research that beats QuaRot — is absent from both the oracle catalog and the worker. For full W4A4 quantization (the FP4 activation regime) this is the recommended rotation method.

Oracle quality_loss values are hardcoded scalars with no provenance and no NVFP4/size-aware curve. The research gives size-dependent recovery (95-98% at 7-14B vs ~99% at 70B+); the oracle's flat per-method loss cannot express that smaller models degrade more under FP4 — the exact failure mode the research flags.

export-nvfp4.js uses modelopt's default NVFP4_DEFAULT_CFG/NVFP4_FP8_KV_CFG and a trivial single-sentence calibration forward_loop (line 232), and does NOT consume the BATQuant plan that fp4_calib.py produces — the two FP4 halves are not fused, so the export does naive modelopt calibration, not the outlier-resilient block-affine pass.

No NVFP4-vs-MXFP4 scale-format distinction surfaced to the user. Research stresses NVFP4's E4M3 block scale beats MXFP4's E8M0 power-of-2 scale; export-nvfp4 only does NVFP4 and fp4-calib-plan treats them identically (block=32 for both, but NVFP4 micro-block is 16).

frontierState: SOTA June 2026 (from tf3 research): KV compression stacks four orthogonal axes, often composed for multiplicative near-lossless gains measured vs FP16. (a) Quantization: KIVI asymmetric 2-bit, per-channel K / per-token V, ~2.6x, ICML2024 arXiv:2402.02750 (Feb2024, repo jy-yuan/KIVI); KVQuant 3-4bit to 10M ctx, NeurIPS2024 arXiv:2401.18079 (repo SqueezeAILab/KVQuant); FP8 E4M3/E5M2 ~2x; Google TurboQuant 3-4bit near-lossless (Mar2026, ICLR2026), 4-bit often indistinguishable from FP16 on >=8B. (b) Eviction/sparsity: StreamingLLM sinks arXiv:2309.17453, H2O arXiv:2306.14048, SnapKV arXiv:2404.14469, PyramidKV arXiv:2406.02069 — 2-8x via attention/importance scoring with sinks/windows; NVIDIA kvpress unifies these as forward hooks. (c) Low-rank: Palu hidden-dim projection ICLR2025 arXiv:2407.21118 (repo shadowpa0327/palu); Eigen Attention arXiv:2408.05646 ~40% KV / ~60% latency cut. (d) Structural: Shard PCA(K, post-RoPE-undo)+Hadamard+VQ(V) with FP16 sinks/windows, ~10x@8K / 11x@32K on Llama-3.1-8B no measurable NIAH/LongBench drop (May2026, github krish1905/shard). Mixed-precision/importance-tiered (Oaken ISCA2025) is orthogonal and stacks. Integration: vLLM PagedAttention natively does FP8 KV + eviction extensions (KV-Compress); SGLang RadixAttention does prefix sharing + HiCache tiering; neither has universal native Shard/Palu. For a platform with an importance-tiered profiler + PCA+VQ compressor but no runtime dispatch, the research names the highest-leverage move as wiring runtime dispatch that uses the profiler to selectively apply PCA+VQ / hybrid precision at allocation time; realistic parity target ~8-12x (centered 10x).
alreadyFrontier: Importance-tiered KV profiler: 7 token classes -> precision tiers (sink/policy/schema/retrieved_evidence/conversation_recent/boilerplate/irrelevant_span) with StreamingLLM-derived sink anchor=4 and citation-confidence-driven tiers (src/itkv-profile.js) - matches the mixed-precision/importance axis the research calls orthogonal and highest-leverage.

Shard PCA(K int4)+Hadamard+VQ256(V) structural contract with FP16 sink/window, ~10x byte-math and VRAM-fit solver (src/kv-cache-shard.js) - mirrors the May2026 SOTA structural method (krish1905/shard) the research ranks highest-ratio.

Full eviction + quant policy registry with the exact SOTA method set StreamingLLM/H2O/SnapKV/PyramidKV/KIVI-2/4 and published defaults, with an honest per-runtime capability gate (eviction=transformers-only, vLLM->kivi2/fp8 fallback) - src/serve-config.js KV_POLICIES + selectKvCachePolicy.

Real Python runtime dispatch on the transformers engine via NVIDIA kvpress presses + transformers HQQQuantizedCache, soft-failing on missing optional deps - apps/runtime/serve.py build_press/quantized_cache_for/_build_kv_cache_for_policy.
vLLM FP8 KV cache on Hopper/Blackwell (capability>=9) + prefix caching + chunked prefill already enabled (serve.py _try_vllm).
Passport schema that can record a policy-aware measured kv_cache sub-object with status tested/estimated (serve-config.js kvPolicyPassportEntry + runtime-passport.js addKvPolicyToPassport).

gaps.gap: CLI dispatch dead-end: `kolm serve` (cli/kolm.js:14302-14347) calls only the legacy binary selectKvCache and sets KOLM_KV_CACHE_BACKEND; it never calls buildServeConfig/selectKvCachePolicy and never sets KOLM_KV_POLICY, and --kv-cache is validated only against [auto,shard,default]. The entire SOTA policy dispatcher (streaming/h2o/snapkv/pyramidkv/kivi2/kivi4) is built and unit-tested but UNREACHABLE from the user-facing command. This is the single thing standing between as-built and frontier.

No measurement: probe.py records the kv_policy NAME (apps/export/probe.py:408,464) but never measures compression_ratio / retained_tokens / evicted_tokens / peak_kv_mb / quality_delta, so every kv_cache passport row stays status:'estimated'. The 'receipt is the proof' claim is unmet for the KV axis; Shard's ~10x and KIVI's quality_delta are asserted, never verified.

Importance profiler not fused into dispatch: itkv-profile.js token-class precision tiers are computed but never feed selectKvCachePolicy or serve.py. The research's explicit highest-leverage move (use the profiler to SELECTIVELY apply PCA+VQ / hybrid precision) is not wired - the profiler and the compressor are two islands.

Sidecar referenced but missing: serve.py build_press (:278) says it 'mirrors workers/itkv/scripts/kvpolicy.py build_press' but neither workers/itkv/scripts/kvpolicy.py nor workers/itkv/requirements.txt exist (only itkv.py). The kvpress dep is not pinned/isolated and the JS<->Python policy parity contract has no Python side.

Low-rank axis (Palu/Eigen Attention) entirely absent from KV_POLICIES; the registry covers quant+eviction+structural(Shard) but not the ~2-4x orthogonal low-rank projection axis that the research lists.

vLLM path gets only fp8 KV; no KVQuant/3-4bit, no KV-Compress paged eviction, no SGLang HiCache tiering - the prod-favored runtime is left at the basic quant axis while the rich policies only land on the transformers/edge path.

frontierState: EAGLE-3 (Li et al, arXiv:2503.01840, Mar 2025; repo SafeAILab/EAGLE) is SOTA: direct token prediction + multi-layer (low/mid/high) feature fusion + training-time-test (TTT) + dynamic draft trees from EAGLE-2 (arXiv:2406.16858). Reports ~3.0-6.5x speedup, ~1.4x over EAGLE-2, mean accepted length ~2-3+. P-EAGLE (parallel drafting, AWS blog, integrated in vLLM ~early 2026) adds up to 1.69x over vanilla EAGLE-3. Support: vLLM v0.8.5+ native (CUDA graphs, mean-acceptance-length metrics, method=eagle3), SGLang (speculative_algorithm EAGLE3), TensorRT-LLM (Eagle3). Runners-up: EAGLE/EAGLE-2 (arXiv:2401.15077 / 2406.16858), Hydra (arXiv:2402.05109, sequentially-dependent draft heads, up to 2.7x), Medusa (arXiv:2401.10774, ~2x). Lookahead decoding (arXiv:2402.02057, hao-ai-lab/LookaheadDecoding) up to 1.8x, zero training. 2025-26: SpecForge (EAGLE-3 training framework), vLLM speculators v0.3.0 (vllm.ai blog 2025-12-13, in-repo speculator/draft-head training), Meta efficient-EAGLE-at-scale (online distillation, TPC up to ~2.9), DVI (competitive with far less data), Mixture-of-Attentions (~9.5% over EAGLE-2). Recommendation for small distilled models on vLLM: EAGLE-3 draft-head trained on the SAME distillation data (target-generated rollouts) using TTT simulation + multi-layer feature fusion; tools SpecForge / vLLM speculators v0.3.

ourState: Our stack is SERVER-SIDE and NATIVE inference, not on-device or in-browser. Real end-to-end pieces: (1) apps/runtime/serve.py boots vLLM (preferred) or transformers fallback behind an OpenAI-compatible HTTP shim, with genuinely wired speculative_config (eagle3 via apps/runtime/eagle3.py, serve.py lines 430-455), KV-policy dispatch (kvpress eviction plus transformers QuantizedCache KIVI, serve.py 274-360, all soft-import), FP8 KV cache on Hopper plus (serve.py 369-378), prefix cache, chunked prefill, multi-LoRA (serve.py 457-471). Real no-GPU self-test (serve.py 1013-1034). (2) src/serve-config.js is a large DETERMINISTIC ORACLE (1341 lines): kernel oracle, KV-policy resolver with per-runtime gates, EAGLE-2/3 resolver, serving-features, multi-LoRA planner. It RECOMMENDS and EMITS launch specs but does not itself run a model. (3) src/serve-autodetect.js maps format plus hardware to runtime (gguf to llama.cpp, safetensors+cuda to vLLM, safetensors+metal to mlx, dot-mlx to mlx); emits docker-compose and k8s; pure routing, no execution. (4) Native runners that DO execute: src/runners/gguf-runner.js spawns llama.cpp llama-cli (real subprocess plus real sha256 of the gguf, gguf-runner.js 76-160); src/runners/onnx-runner.js runs onnxruntime-NODE (server-side, not Web); apps/runtime/backends/local_mlx.py plus src/compute/backends/local-mlx.js spawn mlx_lm.generate on Apple Silicon. (5) Exporters: src/export-mlx.js, src/export-gguf.js, apps/export/executorch.py (real to_edge to_executorch). CRITICAL: IN-BROWSER MODEL EXECUTION DOES NOT EXIST. Browser layer (public/kolm-verify.js, public/verify-widget.js, /verify) only does Ed25519 SIGNATURE verification of a JSON report or receipt via WebCrypto; no WebGPU, no WebLLM, no transformers.js, no model inference. PHANTOM EVIDENCE: src/platform-capabilities.js lines 113-125 (wasm-webgpu row) lists evidence public/device/webgpu-runner.js and webgpu-runner.html but Glob confirms public/device/ does NOT exist and neither file exists anywhere; status softened to in_progress claiming a Minimal transformers.js WebGPU WASM runner shipped but it was never built. browser-webgpu device row (line 217) is status target-declared.
frontierState: As of June 2026 (tf6-ondevice-inference-sota.json), in-browser LLM inference is dominated by WebGPU engines. Leader: WebLLM (MLC-AI, github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm; arXiv:2412.15803v2) OpenAI-compatible, TVM-compiled WebGPU kernels, about 80 percent of native MLC-LLM perf; about 71 tok/s Phi-3.5-mini 4-bit on M3 Max Chrome. New strong entrant: llama.cpp WebGPU also called LlamaWeb (about May 2026, arXiv:2605.20706v1, reeselevine.github.io/llamas-on-the-web) loads GGUF directly in-browser, 54-69 percent faster decode than WebLLM. Also ONNX Runtime Web plus WebGPU EP (good for embeddings, lags big generative). Native on-device: MLX (Apple, best on M-series, 100-200 plus tok/s for 1-3B 4-bit), llama.cpp (GGUF gold standard), ExecuTorch (Meta, GA Oct 2025, 50KB base, CoreML QNN mobile). Formats: WebLLM uses MLC q4f16_1; llama.cpp uses GGUF; MLX uses mlx; ONNX uses onnx; ExecuTorch uses dot-pte. Best path for a verify-the-model-runs-and-is-the-signed-one in-browser demo: WebGPU via WebLLM or LlamaWeb; verify downloaded model bytes signature or hash with Web Crypto (Ed25519 SHA-256) BEFORE handing them to the engine, then run inference. Gotchas: download 0.5-1.5GB (1B) to 1.5-3GB (3B), cache in IndexedDB; cold TTFT seconds to tens of seconds, warm sub-second; WebGPU best on Chrome Edge; OOM on low-RAM; engine must never auto-fetch an unsigned variant.
alreadyFrontier: In-browser Ed25519 signature verification via WebCrypto is real, dependency-free, offline (public/kolm-verify.js) - the crypto half of the frontier verify-in-browser recipe.
Native llama.cpp GGUF execution is wired with real sha256 binding of the model bytes (src/runners/gguf-runner.js lines 101-103) - the is-the-signed-one hash-binding primitive exists server-side.
MLX is correctly identified as the best Apple-Silicon runtime and is wired (export-mlx.js plus local_mlx.py mlx_lm.generate).
Server-side speculative decoding uses the MODERN vLLM speculative_config dict (no deprecated flat kwargs) with EAGLE-2/3 (serve.py 430-455, serve-config.js buildVllmSpeculativeConfig).
Format-to-runtime autodetect (gguf to llama.cpp, safetensors metal to mlx, dot-mlx to mlx) matches the frontier per-format runtime mapping (serve-autodetect.js RUNTIME_SELECTION).

ourState: kolm's verifiable-inference posture is "signed-artifact authenticity + tamper-evident witnessing" (REAL, frontier-grade) but NOT "proven-compute". REAL and strong: (1) src/merkle.js is a correct RFC 6962/9162 inclusion-proof verifier with fn/sn/LSB walk (lines 148-192), computeRoot, inclusionPath, and length-checked timingSafeEqual root compare; header (lines 6-12) documents that it fixes the old broken index%2 / lone-right-edge walk. (2) src/transparency-log.js is a real append-only log: entry_hash=SHA256(prev||body) (line 144), treeHead, C2SP signed-note checkpoints via signTreeHead (line 285), witness co-signing cosignTreeHead/verifyCosignedTreeHead (lines 506-562) for split-view defense, offline verifyInclusionProof (line 406), persisted checkpoints. (3) src/rfc3161-timestamp.js is real: timestampDigest (line 384) does a real TSA POST, verifyTimestamp re-checks messageImprint+genTime+CMS sig vs embedded cert (verifyCmsSignature line 310), selfIssueTimestamp (line 534) for self-issued tokens; trust labeled 'embedded-cert'. (4) src/sigstore.js builds real cosign/Rekor hashedrekord bundles: submitToRekor (line 117), verifyRekorInclusionProof (lines 400-480) with fixed full-length root compare. (5) src/auditor-attestation.js is real third-party Ed25519 attestation with distinct-key enforcement (auditor key != builder key), crossCheckAttestation, build/validateAuditorAttestationBlock. (6) src/attestation-report-builder.js wraps a signed report envelope with canonicalizeReport, an anti-theater buildCaveats section (lines 313-334), and recordTransparencyEntry anchoring the report digest in the log. WEAK / STUB / TOFU: GPU/TEE attestation is shape-only or trust-on-first-use, never crypto-chain-verified. src/confidential-compute.js verifyAttestation returns verifier:'shape_v1', verified:false, reason:'shape_only_stub_no_crypto_chain' (line 229); it models NRAS (NVIDIA Hopper CC) as KIND 'nras' (line 57) with REPORT_SHAPES[NRAS] (lines 104-111) and provider 'nvidia-cc' (line 317), and exposes pluggable registerAttestationVerifier(kind, fn) (line 119) / registerVerifier(provider, fn) (line 326) that NEVER set verified=true without a registered crypto verifier (good safety default). But a repo-wide grep shows NO registerAttestationVerifier('nras', ...) and no real verifier registered anywhere in src/ - production default is shape-only for ALL kinds incl. NRAS; the only verifier present is fakeVerifier (line 343), test-only and self-flagged __fake__. packages/attestation/src/index.js header (lines 13-26) states plainly "Cryptographic verification of the vendor signing chain is stubbed" and "TOFU is acceptable"; packages/attestation/src/tdx.js parses mr_td by byte offset (lines 34-60) but leaves signing_cert_chain: null. src/byoc.js recordAttestation (line 86) parses and TOFU-overrides the operator measurement claim but walks no chain; wired to /v1/byoc/attestation (auth.js:389). src/binder.js check #11 (lines 747-827) re-runs verifyAttestation and honestly surfaces "shape-only (no cryptographic chain walked)". src/devices.js (lines 376-378) advertises tee:'nvidia-cc'/attestation:'nras'. apps/runtime/self_verify.py is an LLM chain-of-verification self-critique loop (self_verify(prompt, candidate, model_fn, max_retries=3)) - inference-time quality scaling only, explicitly NOT proof-of-inference. Tests: merkle/transparency-log/rfc3161/sigstore have real unit tests; packages/attestation/tests/attestation.test.js is synthetic-fixture parsing only, real-hardware tests deferred.
frontierState: SOTA proof-of-inference as of June 2026, ranked by production-readiness (per tf7-verifiable-inference-sota.json): (1) GPU TEE / confidential computing is the only deployable path today - NVIDIA Hopper/Blackwell Confidential Computing (H100/H200) with NRAS (NVIDIA Remote Attestation Service) and nvtrust, producing a signed attestation token nonce-bound to the workload; commercial use by Tinfoil (tinfoil.sh) and Inference Labs. Standard verification = walk the NVIDIA device-identity cert chain to the NVIDIA root, verify the token signature, bind the nonce to the inference output. (2) opML (optimistic ML, arXiv:2401.17555) - run inference normally, post a bond, allow fraud proofs via interactive dispute over a re-executed step; Ora (github.com/ora-io/opml) productionizes it; Gensyn Verde (arXiv:2502.19405) adds refereed-delegation verification for training/inference over decentralized GPUs. (3) zkML - cryptographic proof of correct execution: zkLLM (arXiv:2404.16109, CCS 2024, github.com/jvhs0706/zkllm-ccs2024) proves a 13B model in <15 min, proof <200kB, verify 1-3s; EZKL (github.com/zkonduit/ezkl) general but ~30M-param ceiling; zkGPT (USENIX Security 2025) transformer-specialized circuits. Still too slow/limited for full-size LLMs in production. (4) Sampling/statistical re-execution checks - immature, no security guarantee. tf7 recommendation: load+verify an Ed25519-signed model artifact INSIDE an attested NVIDIA Hopper CC enclave and emit an NRAS token whose nonce commits to the output - i.e., bind kolm's existing signed-artifact provenance to a GPU-TEE compute attestation.
alreadyFrontier: RFC 6962/9162 Merkle inclusion-proof verification done correctly with the lone-right-edge walk bug fixed and a length-checked timingSafeEqual root compare (src/merkle.js lines 148-192)

Append-only transparency log with C2SP signed-note checkpoints and witness co-signing for split-view detection - matches the modern sigsum/Rekor-v2 witness model (src/transparency-log.js signTreeHead line 285, cosignTreeHead/verifyCosignedTreeHead lines 506-562)
Cosign/Rekor-compatible hashedrekord bundle creation and offline inclusion verification (src/sigstore.js submitToRekor line 117, verifyRekorInclusionProof lines 400-480)

RFC 3161 trusted timestamping with full offline re-verification of messageImprint+genTime+CMS signature against the embedded cert (src/rfc3161-timestamp.js verifyTimestamp / verifyCmsSignature line 310)
Independent third-party Ed25519 auditor attestation with enforced distinct-key separation from the builder key and cross-check against the manifest (src/auditor-attestation.js)

Fail-closed safety default: the attestation registry never marks verified=true without a registered cryptographic verifier, and binder check #11 honestly reports 'shape-only (no cryptographic chain walked)' (src/confidential-compute.js line 229, src/binder.js lines 747-827)
Anti-theater caveats surface in the signed report so claims are not overstated (src/attestation-report-builder.js buildCaveats lines 313-334)

NRAS / NVIDIA Hopper CC is already first-classed in the data model and device catalog and the verifier registry is pluggable - the schema and seam for the frontier path already exist (src/confidential-compute.js KIND nras line 57, REPORT_SHAPES lines 104-111, registerAttestationVerifier line 119; src/devices.js lines 376-378)

gaps.gap: No proven-compute capability at all: kolm proves WHAT artifact ran and WHEN it was logged, never THAT a given output was produced by the claimed model on the claimed input. None of the three frontier paths (GPU TEE token verification, opML fraud proofs, zkML) is implemented. This is the core verifiable-inference claim and it is absent.

NRAS is schema-only: NVIDIA Hopper CC attestation is modeled (KIND nras, REPORT_SHAPES, nvidia-cc provider, device catalog entry) but NO verifier is registered, so verifyAttestation returns verified:false reason shape_only_stub_no_crypto_chain for NRAS in production. The one frontier path the codebase is wired for is inert.

TEE attestation parsers are TOFU with stubbed signing chains: packages/attestation/src/index.js documents the vendor cert-chain check as stubbed and TOFU as acceptable; tdx.js leaves signing_cert_chain null; byoc.js recordAttestation TOFU-overrides the operator measurement but walks no chain. A forged/replayed quote is not detected.

src/sigstore.js still hand-rolls its own RFC 6962 leaf/inner hashing and tree walk (rfc6962LeafHash/rfc6962InnerHash, lines 441-458) including a lone-right-edge branch that merkle.js's header explicitly calls the broken pattern it was written to replace; it was never migrated to the audited merkle.verifyInclusion, risking divergent/incorrect inclusion verification.
No opML or zkML tier exists even as a roadmap stub or interface, so there is no untrusted-hardware or fully-cryptographic verification option to complement the TEE path.

No customer-facing disclaimer distinguishing 'signed-artifact provenance + tamper-evident log' from 'proof that this output was actually computed by this model' - a reader could over-read the attestation report as proof-of-inference.

ourState:
kolm has a genuinely strong, mostly-real custom crypto + interop layer, but the standards-aligned pieces are split across two scopes (build-time ARTIFACT vs run-time RECEIPT) and the artifact side is incompletely wired.

REAL and end-to-end:
- DSSE PAE is correctly implemented to the spec (byte-length, not char-length; base64url->base64 re-encode) in src/intoto-slsa.js:69-86, buildDsseEnvelope (251-275), verifyDsseEnvelope (281-337). Byte-exact PAE vector is asserted in tests (tests/wave921-intoto-attestation.test.js, tests/jws-slsa.test.js).
- in-toto v1 Statement + SLSA Provenance v1 predicate builders are real: buildInTotoStatement (intoto-slsa.js:212), buildSlsaProvenancePredicate (153) with required buildType + builder.id, resolvedDependencies from lineage (96-147). emitArtifactAttestation (392-457) produces a full signed DSSE sidecar string. Honest conformance string 'SLSA Provenance v1 (Build L2 shape)' (48).
- Inference-receipt -> in-toto/OMS path is real and tested: signInTotoBundle (intoto-receipt.js:227), toOmsBundle (287) with the web-confirmed OMS predicateType 'https://model_signing/signature/v1.0' (64), subjects as (name,digest) pairs, embedded RFC 8037 OKP JWK + PEM. verifyInTotoBundle (327) with optional subjectDigestMap content check.
- sigstore block on receipts is real and verified offline: buildSigstoreBundle (sigstore.js:197), verifySigstoreBundle (269) including a from-scratch RFC 6962 inclusion-proof verifier (verifyRekorInclusionProof, 400-480) with constant-time full-length root compare. Rekor submit/fetch (117-180) and post-build upgrade attestArtifactWithRekor (533-616) are real network paths with graceful dry-run fallback. Tested: tests/wave150-sigstore.test.js, tests/wave162-sigstore-rekor.test.js.
- Wired into the build: artifact.js:1784 writes signature_sigstore (dry-run) into receipt.json; artifact.js:2098 optionally upgrades via Rekor. .kolm format documented (docs/kolm-format-v1.md), four interop paths documented (docs/crypto-standards.md).

GAPS / STUB-ish (where standards alignment is claimed but not delivered):
- The SLSA in-toto attestation for the .kolm ARTIFACT is NOT emitted into the container at build time. emitArtifactAttestation / buildSlsaProvenance are only reachable through API routes (govern-routes.js:242 POST /v1/govern/provenance/build) and never written as a ZIP member; the header comment (intoto-slsa.js:28) promises a 'provenance.intoto.dsse.json' sidecar but Grep shows no code path appends it to the zip. The only attestation member shipped (artifact.js:1900 attestation_report.json) is a confidential-compute attestation, a different thing.
- The OMS bundle subjects an inference RECEIPT, not the actual model file bytes (model.gguf/safetensors/onnx). The OMS standard's core use is signing the model file manifest; toOmsBundle (intoto-receipt.js:287) maps receipt subjects (receipt digest + output_hash), so it is not an OMS model manifest of the artifact's weight files.
- The 'sigstore bundle' is a hand-shaped JSON object using the Sigstore bundle mediaType string (sigstore.js:53 v0.2; intoto-receipt.js:253 v0.3) but is NOT a sigstore/protobuf-specs bundle and is not Fulcio/cert-based; cosign cannot consume it as-is. The receipt sigstore block (messageSignature form) does not even carry the DSSE/in-toto Statement; only the separate intoto-receipt path does.
- src/slsa-provenance.js referenced in docs/crypto-standards.md is for the ASR audit-report product, separate from the model-artifact path (two parallel SLSA implementations).
- No BLAKE2b digest option (OMS allows it; we are SHA-256 only). No model-card / EU AI Act Annex XI/XII document binding in any predicate.
frontierState: SOTA (mid-2026) is OpenSSF Model Signing (OMS) / Sigstore model-transparency, v1.0 launched 2025-04-04, repos sigstore/model-transparency + ossf/model-signing-spec, library/CLI `model-signing`. Artifact = a DETACHED Sigstore bundle (protobuf, sigstore/protobuf-specs) wrapping a DSSE envelope whose payload is an in-toto Statement; the subject is a MANIFEST listing every file in the model with per-file digests (default SHA-256, BLAKE2b allowed), predicateType 'https://model_signing/signature/v1.0'. PKI-agnostic: Sigstore keyless (Fulcio cert + Rekor inclusion proof) OR bare Ed25519/RSA keys OR enterprise PKI/PKCS#11; offline verification supported when the bundle embeds all material. in-toto/SLSA share the same DSSE+Statement format (slsa.dev provenance/v1, in-toto/attestation). Adoption: NVIDIA signs all NGC models with OMS (~2025-07); Google/Kaggle integrated (case study 2025-07-23). Hugging Face safetensors has NO native origin signing (audit 2023 was deserialization-safety only). EU AI Act Art. 11 (Annex IV) + Art. 53 (Annex XI/XII GPAI) require technical documentation aligning with model cards; voluntary GPAI Code of Practice published 2025-07-10. Recommended attestation stack = Sigstore + SLSA + OCI registries with attached bundles/SBOMs (SPDX/CycloneDX AI-BOM emerging). Cited: openssf.org OMS spec intro 2025-06-25; github.com/ossf/model-signing-spec; github.com/sigstore/model-transparency; next.redhat.com 2025-04-10; sigstore BUNDLE_SPEC.md.
alreadyFrontier: Correct DSSE PAE (byte-length, base64url->std-base64) with a byte-exact spec vector asserted in tests — this is the #1 cosign interop trap and kolm gets it right (src/intoto-slsa.js:69-86 + tests/wave921, tests/jws-slsa)
in-toto v1 Statement + SLSA Provenance v1 predicate with required fields (buildType, builder.id) and honest 'Build L2 shape' conformance, never over-claiming L3 (src/intoto-slsa.js:48,153,212)

OMS-shaped DSSE bundle with the web-confirmed OMS predicateType 'https://model_signing/signature/v1.0' and embedded RFC 8037 OKP JWK + PEM, bare-key (non-Fulcio) mode stated plainly (src/intoto-receipt.js:64,252-266,287)

From-scratch RFC 6962 Merkle inclusion-proof verification with constant-time full-length root compare, plus Rekor submit/fetch and graceful dry-run fallback so builds never block on Rekor uptime (src/sigstore.js:400-480,117-180,244-261)

Offline-first verification preserved (kolm verify works without network/account) and multiple parallel interop formats documented and implemented (Ed25519, JWS/JOSE, DSSE/in-toto, RFC9162+RFC3161) per docs/crypto-standards.md
Zero-heavy-deps: PAE/DSSE/Merkle all vanilla node:crypto, avoiding @sigstore/* transitive trees — a deliberate, defensible design choice

gaps.gap: No OMS/SLSA attestation is written INTO the .kolm artifact at build time. emitArtifactAttestation/buildSlsaProvenance exist and are tested but are only reachable via an API route (govern-routes.js:242); the promised provenance.intoto.dsse.json sidecar (intoto-slsa.js:28) is never appended to the ZIP. A buyer who receives a .kolm file cannot run `cosign verify-attestation` or `model-signing verify` on it because the standards artifact is not in the container.

The OMS bundle subjects an inference receipt, not the model's actual file bytes (model.gguf/model.safetensors/model.onnx). OMS's defining purpose is a signed per-file manifest of the model weights; kolm never produces an OMS manifest over the artifact's weight members, so the 'OMS-compatible' claim does not cover the canonical OMS use case.

The receipt 'sigstore bundle' is a hand-shaped JSON using the Sigstore bundle mediaType string but is not a sigstore/protobuf-specs bundle and carries only a messageSignature (no DSSE/in-toto Statement); cosign cannot ingest it. It mislabels itself with the official mediaType (application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle+json;version=0.2), which can confuse strict tooling.

Two parallel SLSA implementations exist (src/slsa-provenance.js for the ASR report product vs src/intoto-slsa.js for model artifacts), increasing drift risk and making 'which one is canonical' ambiguous for the model platform.

SHA-256 only; OMS permits BLAKE2b. No Fulcio/keyless (OIDC identity) option, so SLSA Build L3 and verified-publisher identity binding are out of reach. No model-card / EU AI Act Annex XI-XII document digest bound into any predicate.

SOTA June 2026 (from tf9-moe-distill-quant-sota.json):
(1) MoE->dense distillation: krafton-ai/moe-to-dense (arXiv ~May/Jun 2026) - first systematic framework: score experts (best = diversity-aware DO-ACP / D-optimal on activation-weighted conditional prob, Gram matrices), select/group/merge (pure prune or weighted avg), concat into a DENSE FFN, then forward-KL logit distill. Qwen3-30B-A3B (128 experts, top-8) -> ~3.3B dense; DO-ACP prune + ~4B-token distill beats dense->dense pruning by +6.3pp avg, 1.6x faster. SlimMoE (HF Jun 2025): multi-stage expert-slimming + staged distill, Phi-3.5-MoE 41.9B->Phi-mini-MoE 7.6B / Phi-tiny-MoE 3.8B on only 400B tokens. DeepSeek-R1 distills (R1-0528-Qwen3-8B beats base 8B ~10%).
(2) Expert-aware quant: MC-MoE/Mixture-Compressor (ICLR2025, TPAMI2026) 1.5-2.5-bit weight-only; GEMQ (ICLR2026) global expert-level mixed precision; DynaExq (arXiv Feb 2026, 2511.15015) runtime budget-constrained precision allocation; ACL2025-findings channel-wise per-expert quant +3.96% on Mixtral-8x7B; QuantMoE-Bench.
(3) Serving: Expert Parallelism (vLLM/SGLang EP, Wide-EP GB200 1.8x/GPU, NVIDIA Hybrid-EP 514% on DeepSeek-V3/Qwen3-235B, Semantic Parallelism in SGLang); expert offloading (FineMoE EuroSys26, MoE-Infinity, FloE - 3-20x latency, ~70% mem savings).
Best 1T->laptop pipeline: expert score (DO-ACP) -> structural prune/merge to dense ~7-13B -> staged distill (LM warmup then forward-KL, optional on-policy) -> expert-aware low-bit quant. Realistic retention 70-90%+ of teacher reasoning (parity on math/code) for a tuned 8-13B dense student.
alreadyFrontier: Real forward/reverse-KL + CE logit distillation with temperature, top-k and on-policy hooks (apps/trainer/distill.py) - the KD objective half of moe-to-dense is already SOTA-grade and is the exact loss krafton uses post-merge.

Accurate MoE topology detection across all current frontier families incl. DeepSeek-V3, Qwen3-MoE, Llama4 (src/moe-support.js detectMoE + moe-registry.js) - the prerequisite metadata layer for any MoE compression is solid and tested.

gaps.gap: No MoE-teacher -> dense-student path exists at all. The single most important frontier ask (collapse a 1T-class MoE reasoner into a laptop dense student) has zero code: no expert merge/concat-into-dense-FFN, no DO-ACP/D-optimal scoring, no staged prune-then-distill orchestration. recommendArch can EMIT a dense student size but nothing collapses MoE expert weights into it.

frontierState:
SOTA per research/strategy-2026/raw-tech-frontier/tf10-llm-routing-sota.json (June 2026):
- vLLM Semantic Router v0.1 "Iris" (vllm-project/semantic-router; Sept 2025, Iris Jan 2026; arXiv 2603.04444): multi-signal MoM router (ModernBERT classifiers + heuristics + embeddings + safety), composable Boolean plugin chains, semantic caching, jailbreak/PII, signal-decision architecture (vllm.ai 2025-11-19). The clearest 2025-26 multi-signal production leader.
- RouteLLM (lm-sys/RouteLLM; arXiv 2406.18665, ICLR 2025): preference-trained lightweight routers (similarity-weighted Elo / matrix factorization) thresholded on cost; ~40%+ cost savings at quality parity on MT-Bench.
- NotDiamond (RoRF random-forest, github Not-Diamond/RoRF) + Martian (RouterBench, arXiv 2403.12031): commercial predicted-quality routers trained on per-query eval data; 20-97% cost cuts.
- Cascade/escalation unified with routing: "cascade routing" (ICML 2025, openreview AAl89VNNy1); Router-R1 (NeurIPS 2025) RL multi-round routing+aggregation with cost-aware rewards. Escalation rate is the key monitored variable.
- RouterWise (arXiv 2604.10907, 2026-04, cited in our own code): per-model latency is a function of request LOAD on the shared pool -> load is a first-class routing signal.
The research's prescribed simplest-beats-static design: persist embedding+intent, predicted quality, ACTUAL quality labels (LLM-judge/schema-validity/feedback), cost, model+outcome+escalation, per-(intent,model) history; then a small predicted-quality router that picks the cheapest model whose predicted quality >= bar, with a cascade fallback, retrained periodically on persisted labels.
alreadyFrontier: Auditable, signed-receipt routing decision block (buildRouterDecisionBlock, semantic-router.js:885) recording chosen/rejected/signals/weights - this provenance edge is AHEAD of vLLM-SR/RouteLLM/Martian which do not ship per-request signed routing provenance.

Multi-signal weighted blend over quality/cost/latency/load/similarity with per-candidate weight renormalization (blendSignals, semantic-router.js:170) - matches the vLLM signal-decision architecture conceptually, including RouterWise's load signal which we cite and model.

Confidence/entropy cascade escalation (runtime-confidence-router.js decideRouting) with mid-stream teacher splicing (confidence-router.js) - this IS the cascade/escalation frontier pattern, and it is genuinely wired (accelerate.js).

The exact label/signal persistence the research prescribes is implemented: route-quality-store.js persists embedding-context + realized quality + cost + latency + win label per (cluster,model), tenant-fenced, in ClusterRouterStats.restore shape.
Anytime-valid sequential A/B gates (GAVI/mSPRT, ab-router.js sequentialDecision) for peeking-safe promote/rollback - stronger statistical machinery than RouterBench's fixed-horizon evals.
Training-free clustering router (Avengers-Pro) chosen deliberately over a trained neural router, consistent with LLMRouterBench 2026 finding that clustering matches trained routers.

ourState:
The KOLM data engine is a REAL, end-to-end, dependency-free curation+augmentation pipeline, not a stub. orchestratePipeline (src/data-engine.js:336) chains INGEST→CURATE→AUGMENT→EVALUATE→FEEDBACK with per-stage try/catch isolation and a cost gate on AUGMENT (preview-only unless opts.approve_cost_usd >= est_cost_usd, src/data-engine.js:202-218).

CURATE (src/data-curate.js:375 curatePairs) runs six gated stages, all really implemented: quality (output-only heuristic scoreCandidateLocal, src/data-curate.js:163; OR opt-in learned logistic classifier), minhash pre-dedup, python semantic dedup (degrades to no-op), cluster, label-error detect, CoT-drop, PII-redact, and diversity SELECT. The W921 frontier modules are genuine algorithms:
- src/minhash-dedup.js: real Broder MinHash + LSH banding + union-find + datasketch-style optimalBands (S-curve FP/FN integral, minhash-dedup.js:330) + true-Jaccard verify pass; deterministic dedup_signature receipt. Tested (tests/wave921-data-curate-modules.test.js).
- src/data-select.js + src/data-diversity-select.js: real k-center-greedy (Sener&Savarese), facility-location lazy-greedy submodular (Minoux), BADGE weighted k-means++, and DSIR centroid-importance resampling. Tested.
- src/data-quality-classifier.js: real logistic head (sigmoid(w·x+b)) with deterministic batch GD fit + DCLM percentile / absolute thresholding + shipped cold-start weights (FineWeb-Edu/DCLM/AlpaGasus lineage).
- src/data-label-errors.js: real Confident-Learning confident-joint (Northcutt JAIR'21) over input-cluster→output-cluster agreement, plus CLEAR/BSDetector teacher path (injectable sampler). 
- src/data-cluster-label.js: real k-means++ + Lloyd + silhouette auto-k + c-TF-IDF slug labeling (BERTopic lineage).

CRITICAL CAVEAT: every embedding is the deterministic 256-d hash-bag embedder (src/embedding.js:38) — bag-of-char-3/4-grams + unigrams, NOT a learned semantic model. So all 'semantic' dedup/clustering/diversity/label-error is lexical, not true semantic. The file itself says 'Swap for a real embedding model when scaling' (embedding.js:3).

AUGMENT (src/data-augment.js:347) is TEMPLATED ONLY — five strategies (gap-fill/evol/persona/adversarial/doc-update) emit prompt strings with empty output:'' awaiting a later teacher collect step. There are NO teacher calls and NO Magpie self-synthesis in the JS engine; previewCost (data-augment.js:293) only prices a hypothetical batch.

Near-paraphrases with low lexical overlap escape dedup; clusters are lexical not topical; DEITA/MIG-style gains are capped because the embedding space does not reflect meaning. This is the single biggest quality ceiling.

Active selection uses PROXY uncertainty (routing entropy at capture time + token-density/novelty importance), NOT the student model's actual per-example prediction loss on candidate data. The tf12 key paper (arXiv:2512.00884) shows student-loss argmax selection is the SOTA criterion and beats reward/entropy proxies. We never recompute student loss on candidates to re-rank the next distill batch.

AUGMENT is templated-string only (data-augment.js): no Magpie self-synthesis in the engine, no actual teacher generation, no LLM-driven Auto-Evol rule design. The frontier's highest-ROI move (selective teacher generate+filter on curated prompts) is a manual two-step, not an engine stage. apps/data/synth.py has the generators but is not wired into orchestratePipeline.

Quality scoring default path is an output-only heuristic (length/CoT/refusal/structure); the learned classifier is opt-in and ships cold-start weights with no per-tenant LLM-judge label bootstrap. There is no DEITA-style LLM complexity scorer or MIG information-gain selection (graph-based), which the research ranks above pointwise quality.

Copyright filter (src/capture-copyright-filter.js) is a ~25-phrase literal denylist + comma-density prose heuristic — far below NVIDIA NeMo license-compliant pipeline standards; high false-negative rate on real copyrighted prose.

ourState:
kolm has a genuinely substantial, mostly-REAL agent-security-eval stack, but it is log-analysis-first, not benchmark-first.

REAL / end-to-end:
- Audit-trail tamper-evidence: src/audit.js implements an append-only per-tenant HMAC-SHA256 chain (appendAudit L110-155, event_hash=HMAC(prev||canonicalPayload) L138; verifyAuditChain L185-206 recomputes and reports breaks; canonicalisePayload L82-97 sorts keys for byte-stable hashing; concurrency fixed with withTransaction/BEGIN IMMEDIATE L128). CLI `kolm audit verify` + /v1/audit/verify consume it. This is the dimension the research says is least covered by public benchmarks - we ship it for real.
- Passive deterministic red-team battery: src/red-team.js, 12 CORE_PROBES (L464-625) + finance/healthcare DOMAIN_PROBES (L627-652) over normalized AuditEvents. Each probe returns resisted/exposed/untested with evidence ids and an OWASP-LLM/Agentic + MITRE-ATLAS framework map (e.g. L469, L483). Graduated severity-weighted score over EXERCISED probes only (_score L658-673), null when nothing exercised. Honest untested discipline throughout (e.g. unicode probe L515 returns untested on clean tokens, never a fake pass). Backed by classifyScopeTier (src/audit-event.js L130-160, token-anchored, real) and secret/unicode/discovery/guardrail heuristics. Tests: tests/red-team.test.js.
- Active (offensive) battery: src/active-redteam.js runActiveBattery (L226) sends armed adversarial prompts to a CONSENTED staging endpoint, classifies live responses via canary-echo/tool_calls (classifyResponse L161-176), consent gate throws CONSENT_REQUIRED before any byte (assertConsent L138-158), no-raw-content (transcript_digest only). Reproducible fixed seed. mergeActiveResults (red-team.js L757-816) folds active into passive (worst-wins). Tests: tests/active-redteam.test.js.
- Orchestrator: src/audit-orchestrator.js runAudit (L172-340) chains ingest -> permission/audit-trail/egress/provenance/identity/rag-memory/delegation analyzers -> mapControls -> red_team, with explicit non-inflation readiness rollup (untested excluded from denominator L275-282) and an A/B/C evidence tier (computeEvidenceTier L117-149). Signed via attestation-report-builder.js (buildRedTeamBlock L386-394, red_team in signed envelope L761).
- Adversarial corpus + classifier: src/adversarial-prompts.js (8 categories, deterministic generator + heuristic classifier L293) and src/adversarial-eval.js (templated weak-cluster probe JSONL).

STUB / NOT wired:
- src/adversarial-bakeoff.js runAdversarialBakeoff is real logic but the HOSTED route ships runOnArtifact:null (router.js L24528) -> returns {ok:false,error:'runtime_not_wired'}. Only tests/self-hosted operators inject the runtime.
- runActiveBattery + mergeActiveResults are NOT wired into any API route or the report builder - only invoked by scripts/audit-active-redteam.mjs (Grep across src: only red-team.js and active-redteam.js reference them). The $10k Deep Red-Team deliverable is operator-CLI-only.
- ACTIVE_PROBES has only 5 ids (active-redteam.js L60-66) vs 12 CORE_PROBES, so active evidence can upgrade at most 5 of the passive probes.
- No public-benchmark adapter exists anywhere in source: Grep for agentdojo/injecagent/agentharm/grayswan hits ONLY research JSON, never src/ or tests/.
frontierState:
SOTA (June 2026) for evaluating agent security is dynamic, sandboxed, task-based eval measuring benign-utility, utility-under-attack, and attack-success-rate (ASR), not static classification:
- AgentDojo (ETH Zurich, arXiv:2406.13352, Jun 2024; NeurIPS 2024): 97 realistic tasks + 629 security test cases over tool-using agents on untrusted data. NIST/US-AISI fork AgentDojo-Inspect (github.com/usnistgov/agentdojo-inspect) added mass-exfil + terminal/RCE envs. NIST red-team (Jan 2025): ~11% baseline task-hijack ASR rising to ~80% with optimized attacks on Claude 3.5 Sonnet.
- InjecAgent (UIUC, arXiv:2403.02691): 1,054 IPI cases, 17 user + 62 attacker tools, direct-harm vs data-exfil intents; ReAct GPT-4 ~24% vulnerable, ~2x with enhanced hacking prompt.
- AgentHarm (Gray Swan + UK AISI, arXiv:2410.09024, ICLR 2025): 110/440 malicious multi-step tasks, 11 harm categories; measures refusal vs tool-backed harmful execution + jailbreak robustness.
- Agent Security Bench / ASB (openreview V4y0CpX4hK): ~84% avg ASR across attacks, limited defense efficacy.
- Gray Swan Arena / ART benchmark (arXiv:2507.20526): massive dynamic red-teaming (272k+ attacks on 13 frontier models, none fully clean).
- Taxonomies: OWASP LLM Top 10 (LLM01 injection, LLM02 disclosure, LLM06 excessive agency) + OWASP Agentic Top 10 2026 (ASI01 goal hijack, ASI02 tool misuse, ASI03 identity/privilege abuse); MITRE ATLAS (~16 tactics/170 techniques incl. AML.T0051 prompt injection, T0057 data leakage). NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative (Feb 2026).
Research verdict for a signed Agent Security Readiness report: map injection-resistance->AgentDojo+InjecAgent, exfil->AgentDojo(+NIST mass-exfil)+InjecAgent, least-privilege/tool-misuse->AgentHarm+OWASP ASI02/03; audit-trail tamper-evidence has NO defensible public benchmark - disclose as proprietary/custom control. Most defensible to INCLUDE: AgentDojo-based injection+exfil+utility. Honestly EXCLUDE/qualify: comprehensive audit-trail tamper-evidence benchmark parity.
alreadyFrontier: Audit-trail tamper-evidence (src/audit.js): a real per-tenant HMAC-SHA256 append-only chain with deterministic canonicalization and a verifier - exactly the control the research says public agent benchmarks do NOT cover, so we are ahead-of-benchmark here and the research advice (disclose as proprietary/custom) matches our framing.
OWASP-LLM/Agentic + MITRE-ATLAS framework mapping baked into every probe (red-team.js frameworks arrays, e.g. L469/L483/L562) - the exact credibility mapping the research recommends.

Honest untested/non-inflation discipline (red-team.js _score over exercised probes only L658-673; orchestrator excludes untested from denominator L275-282) - aligns with frontier insistence that absence-of-attack is not proof-of-resistance and that scores must be coverage-explicit.

Consented, reproducible ACTIVE injection probing with a hard consent gate and no-raw-content digests (active-redteam.js) - matches the offensive red-team posture of Gray Swan / NIST work, with stronger contractual hygiene than most.
Domain-specialized probes (finance money-movement, healthcare PHI exfil) - finer-grained than generic AgentDojo suites for vertical buyers.
Signed, versioned, evidence-tiered deliverable (attestation-report-builder.js + Ed25519/RFC3161 per orchestrator NOT_ASSESSED L65) - the report-as-product the research describes.

gaps.gap: No public-benchmark adapter at all. The research is explicit that credibility for a sold readiness report comes from mapping checks to AgentDojo / InjecAgent / AgentHarm, yet there is zero code that runs or even reports against these suites (Grep: benchmark names appear only in research JSON). We map to the OWASP/ATLAS *taxonomy* but never to the reproducible public *task suites* buyers can compare against.

Active red-team (the only path that produces real attack-success evidence rather than log inference) is not wired into the report builder or any API route - only scripts/audit-active-redteam.mjs. The $10k Deep Red-Team tier therefore cannot be produced through the product surface; it is operator-CLI-only.

Adversarial bakeoff hosted route is inert: runOnArtifact:null (router.js L24528) -> runtime_not_wired. The 'is this compiled student robust to attack framings' product claim has no hosted execution path.

Active-probe coverage asymmetry: 5 ACTIVE_PROBES vs 12 CORE_PROBES, so 7 passive probes (tool-arg-escalation, mcp-discovery, runtime-guardrails-absent, unbounded-tool-calls, credential-in-log, exfil-to-untrusted-host, domain) can never be corroborated by live evidence.

No benign-utility / utility-under-attack axis. Frontier evals report task success AND ASR on the same sandbox; kolm reports resistance only, so it cannot express the utility/security tradeoff that AgentDojo centers.

Heuristic-only injection classifier and refusal scorer (adversarial-prompts.js _PATTERNS L252-275; adversarial-bakeoff.js REFUSAL_KEYWORDS L43-64) with no judge-model default and no quantified false-pos/neg rate - the modules say so honestly but there is no measured calibration against a labeled set.

No mapping of our 12 probe ids to specific public-benchmark case classes (e.g. InjecAgent data-exfil intent, AgentDojo workspace mass-exfil), which is what would let a buyer cross-walk our score to cited literature.

ourState:
Kolm has a genuinely end-to-end "capture API traffic -> distill -> own a signed model" loop, most of it real code rather than stubs.

CAPTURE (real): src/capture.js implements drop-in Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter proxies (forwardAnthropic/forwardOpenAI/forwardOpenRouter, capture.js:283-358) with hardened fetch, body-shape normalization, and a Vercel teacher-chat key-fallback (_proxyViaTeacherChat, capture.js:175-277). It extracts prompt/completion/reasoning traces incl. Anthropic thinking, OpenAI reasoning_tokens, DeepSeek <think>, Gemini thinking (autoDetectReasoningCapability, capture.js:519-587). router.js mounts /v1/capture/{anthropic,openai,openrouter,gemini} proxies (router.js:5976-5991, 17931+) and /v1/capture/log batch ingest (router.js:16908) through recordCapture (router.js:16793). Live SSE tail in capture-stream.js (publishCapture, in-process only, single-instance, capture-stream.js:39-58 - explicitly NOT cross-instance).

DISTILL (real, tiered honesty): src/distill-pipeline.js prepareDistillCorpus pairs prompt->response from the event store with tenant fencing + holdout fail-closed chokepoints (distill-pipeline.js:169-295,526-533). distill() spawns workers/distill/distill.mjs with modes stub/collect/full; full LoRA only runs when python+torch detected, otherwise honest 'collect' (collects teacher pairs) or 'stub' (distill-pipeline.js:420-427, distill.mjs:1-62). Real training exists: apps/trainer/trainer_real.py does Unsloth+PEFT QLoRA with Liger/FlashAttention/8-bit/DPO (trainer_real.py:1-60). Teacher-source policy classifies open-weights vs proprietary and can hard-deny proprietary teachers (classifyTeacher + KOLM_TEACHER_SOURCE, distill-pipeline.js:67-358) - a real TOS-risk guard competitors lack. W459 teacher fallback, W718 teacher council, W808 regression gate all wired (distill-pipeline.js:472-1122). distill-bridge.js wires /v1/distill/from-captures + /v1/specialists/auto-distill to the worker, always returns job_id never 503 (distill-bridge.js:111-222). /v1/distill/from-captures route auto-picks recipe vs specialist by capture count (router.js:16310-16399).

SIGNED EVIDENCE (real, the differentiator): artifact.js builds a .kolm with a 5-step HMAC receipt chain + signature.sig binding artifact_hash/eval_set_hash/eval_score, verifiable via /v1/receipts/verify (artifact.js:22-23,150-151,330-341); optional sigstore (src/sigstore.js). spec-compile.js compileSpec measures accuracy on a real holdout split (seed gate, not claimed), records seed_provenance/distill_provenance/export_provenance/teacher lineage/runtime passports into the bound manifest (spec-compile.js:146-1000+).

PORTABLE/SELF-HOST (real): apps/export + apps/import handle gguf/onnx/safetensors/coreml/tensorrt/executorch; runtime passports + serve.py.

STUB/GAPS: src/cloud-distill.js managed cloud pool is an honest stub - submitJob queues and returns cloud_backend_status='no_pool_configured' until KOLM_CLOUD_DISTILL_ENDPOINT points at a real pool (cloud-distill.js:13-203). No Kolm-hosted training/inference fleet ships; distill assumes the user has local/their-own GPU or wires an external KOLM_TRAINER_BRIDGE_URL. distill() progress events in stub/collect mode are SYNTHETIC k/loss (distill-pipeline.js:722-738) - real loss curves only in full mode. No no-code Studio UI; no continuous-RL loop comparable to OpenPipe ART.
frontierState:
SOTA June 2026 for capture->distill->self-host (from tf14 research):
- OpenPipe (openpipe.ai) is the closest competitor: captures/analyzes LLM logs incl. API traffic, fine-tunes + continuous-RL via open-source ART (Agent Reinforcement Trainer), on-prem/VPC self-host where zero data/weights leave the network, portable models, ~$0.48-2.90/1M training tokens + hosted inference, claims up to 8x lower inference cost. Strongest "set-and-forget proxy -> owned model" story but no signed provenance artifact.
- Predibase: fine-tune UI+SDK + serving of SLMs, dynamic adapter load/unload, no native API-traffic proxy (user supplies data), portable weights.
- Together AI: SFT/LoRA/DPO billed per-1M-tokens ($0.48-2.90+), dedicated endpoints, exportable; reportedly $100M+ ARR with fine-tuning a key driver. No auto-proxy.
- Fireworks AI: fast inference + self-serve SFT/LoRA + RL via FireOptimizer, OpenAI-compatible data, dedicated endpoints. No auto-proxy.
- Modal: serverless GPU to run your own training (Unsloth/Axolotl/TRL), fully portable, pay-per-use; no capture service.
- Baseten/Truss: managed serving + FT infra, replica-hour pricing, portable.
- Unsloth (product): 2x faster/70% less VRAM fine-tune lib + new Unsloth Studio web UI, exports safetensors/GGUF for vLLM/Ollama/llama.cpp; no proxy/capture.
- Nebius Token Factory: post-training/distillation service, token-priced distilled variants.
General trend: distillation from teacher synthetic data into smaller self-hostable students. The research's explicit verdict: a "point API calls at us, own the resulting model, with a signed evidence artifact" platform is genuinely differentiated on (a) automatic live-traffic proxy+capture and (b) cryptographic signed provenance of teacher/data/process - few/none combine both; OpenPipe is closest but has no signed artifact. We are behind on continuous-RL loops, low-latency serving, no-code Studio UX, pricing transparency, and ecosystem/traction.
alreadyFrontier: Automatic live-traffic capture proxy for Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter/Gemini (drop-in base_url swap) - the lower-friction half of the differentiator OpenPipe is closest on (capture.js:283-358, router.js:5976-5991).

Cryptographic signed evidence artifact (.kolm with 5-step HMAC receipt chain + signature.sig + optional sigstore, holdout-measured accuracy, full teacher/data/export lineage) - the part the research says is NOT standard anywhere else (artifact.js:22-341, spec-compile.js seed gate + provenance blocks).

Teacher-source TOS-risk classification with hard-deny of proprietary teachers (classifyTeacher + KOLM_TEACHER_SOURCE open-weights enforcement, distill-pipeline.js:67-358) - a provenance/compliance guard competitors do not surface.
Reasoning-trace distillation capture across Anthropic thinking / OpenAI reasoning_tokens / DeepSeek <think> / Gemini thinking (capture.js:387-605) - matches frontier CoT-distillation trend.
Portable/self-hostable outputs: real export+import to gguf/onnx/safetensors/coreml/tensorrt/executorch + runtime passports (apps/export, apps/import) - parity with Unsloth/Modal portability.
Real LoRA/QLoRA training stack with Unsloth+PEFT+Liger+FlashAttention-2/3+8-bit+DPO when GPU present (apps/trainer/trainer_real.py) - same engine class as the field.
Honesty contract on capability state (cloud pool returns no_pool_configured; stub/collect/full modes are explicit) - avoids the over-claiming the research flags as a competitor DX weakness.

gaps.gap: No Kolm-hosted training/inference fleet: cloud-distill.js is an honest stub (no_pool_configured) and there is no managed GPU pool, so the 'upload captures -> we distill -> download .kolm' promise on /cloud.html cannot actually execute end-to-end without the user bringing their own GPU or an external KOLM_TRAINER_BRIDGE_URL. OpenPipe/Together/Fireworks/Modal/Baseten all run the training+serving for you. This is the single biggest DX gap vs every named competitor.

No continuous-RL / online-optimization loop comparable to OpenPipe ART. We have capture->distill->regression-gate (W808) but no automated reinforcement loop that keeps improving the student from ongoing production traffic.

Synthetic progress telemetry in stub/collect mode: distill() emits fabricated k/loss step events (distill-pipeline.js:722-738). Real loss curves only exist in full mode. A buyer watching /v1/distill/runs in collect mode sees a synthetic curve, which undercuts the evidence/trust positioning if not clearly labeled.
No no-code Studio UI for the distill loop (Unsloth Studio, Predibase UI are the bar). Kolm's loop is API/CLI-first.

Live capture SSE tail is in-process/single-instance only (capture-stream.js:16-19) - no cross-instance pubsub, so on a multi-replica hosted deploy the live tail silently misses captures landing on other instances.
Per-token training/inference pricing transparency lags Together/Fireworks/OpenPipe published rates; cloud-distill.js has a GPU-hour rate table but it is for a pool that does not exist yet.

improvements.what: Define an automated loop that periodically re-distills/RL-tunes the student from fresh production captures, gated by the existing W808 regression gate and signed into a superseding .kolm via the existing drift-supersession blocks. Closes the major feature gap vs OpenPipe's continuous RL while keeping the signed-evidence differentiator.

ourState: kolm has a genuinely REAL, end-to-end signed MCP tool-call receipt path - not a stub - plus a separate MCP server. (1) src/mcp-gateway.js is fully implemented: wrapToolCall (lines 324-365) resolves a tool result (precomputed `result` or injected `execute({tool,args,tenant})`), hashes args+result over canonical JSON (hashMcpArgs L98, hashMcpResult L108, both via cid.canonicalJson so order-independent + tamper-flipping), builds an `mcp-tool-call-1` receipt binding {schema,call_id,timestamp,tenant_id,tool,args_hash,result_hash,is_error,transport,server_id} (MCP_SIGNED_FIELDS L67-78), signs with Ed25519 (signMcpReceipt L245 reusing buildSignatureBlock + loadOrCreateDefaultSigner from src/ed25519.js), and returns a `result_passthrough_contract` asserting the bytes are unaltered + bound by result_hash. verifyMcpReceipt (L278) recomputes the canonical signed subset and checks the signature; canonicalForSigning (L223) strips signature_ed25519. (2) Routes are REAL: src/mcp-gateway-routes.js POST /v1/mcp/dispatch (L140) + GET /v1/mcp/verify/:id (L198), tenant-fenced on req.tenant_record.id (never body), registered in src/router.js L28624 via __registerMcpGatewayRoutes_w921(r,{authMiddleware}). (3) Strong test coverage: tests/wave921-mcp-signed-receipts.test.js has 19 tests covering content, sign/verify, args/result/tenant/tool/is_error/ts tamper, key-swap rejection, canonical determinism, passthrough, executor, and the dispatch->verify route round-trip + cross-tenant 404. (4) An input guardrail exists (src/gateway-guardrail.js) - heuristic prompt-injection classifier with detect_only/flag/block modes, additive non-signed receipt.guardrail field, and a Phase-2 out-of-process transformer seam (classifyTransformer L414, maxPoolChunks L368). (5) in-toto/SLSA + OpenSSF Model-Signing export (src/intoto-receipt.js) and Merkle batch anchoring with RFC6962/9162 inclusion proofs + signed checkpoints (src/transparency-anchor.js, wired into govern-routes anchorBatch L132). REAL GAPS / STUB-ish wiring: (a) MCP gateway is registered with ONLY {authMiddleware} (router L28624) - no `store`, no `getSigner`, no `execute`. So in prod the receipts persist only to a process-local Map (_makePersistence L97-106), lost on restart, and GET /v1/mcp/verify/:id 404s after a redeploy; and with no `execute` the gateway cannot actually invoke an upstream MCP server - it only records receipts for caller-supplied precomputed results (L164/L333). (b) The Merkle anchoring + in-toto export operate on `kolm-audit-1` receipts only (intoto-receipt.js buildInferencePredicate L156 copies receipt_id/namespace_id/route_decision/... - it does not understand mcp-tool-call-1's call_id/tool/args_hash). MCP receipts are NOT enqueued to transparency-anchor and cannot be exported as in-toto - the existence/Merkle proof + supply-chain interop stops at inference receipts. (c) The guardrail is wired into /v1/gateway/dispatch (router L6676) and into the inference path, but NOT into /v1/mcp/dispatch or wrapToolCall - tool-poisoning / injection-via-tool-output is never screened on the MCP path. (d) No tool-description / tool-metadata pinning or rug-pull detection anywhere (no hash of inputSchema/description at registration time). (e) intoto-receipt-routes also registered with only {authMiddleware} (router L28623) so its receipt-resolution seam is unwired in prod too.
frontierState: MCP (Anthropic, Nov 2024; spec rev 2025-06-18) is the dominant tool-call standard with ~10K public servers. Security frontier centers on tool-poisoning attacks (TPA / indirect prompt injection via tool descriptions, schemas AND outputs) - Invariant Labs (Apr 2025), OWASP MCP Tool Poisoning, Simon Willison (Apr 2025), CyberArk 'Poison Everywhere' (no output is safe), Microsoft indirect-injection guidance, Unit42/Tenable. The enterprise control plane is the MCP GATEWAY/proxy: Microsoft Azure AI Gateway (preview May 2026), Portkey MCP Gateway, Kong AI Gateway MCP Tool ACLs v3.13 (Jan 2026), MCP Manager (SOC2 exportable audit logs), Permit MCP Gateway (per-tool trust levels), TrueFoundry, Red Hat/Kuadrant (Envoy + signed JWT 'wristbands', Dec 2025), fiberplane/mcp-gateway (OSS), Solo.io/Christian Posta registration-workflow with cryptographically signed tool metadata. SIGNED RECEIPTS specifically: Signet MCP Server (prismer-ai, mcpservers.org) - Ed25519-signs every tool call, externally-verifiable tamper-evident receipts; KYA/Vouched (kya.vouched.id) - Ed25519 tamper-evident audit trail per tool call, no client opt-in, OSS donated to DIF; HN discussions of per-call Ed25519 receipts with server co-signatures. No dominant OPEN STANDARD for verifiable invocation yet, and per the research almost nobody combines Merkle batch/chained verifiability with the per-call Ed25519 receipt - which is exactly kolm's differentiator. Anchoring frontier = RFC 6962/9162 transparency logs (Sigstore/Rekor) and OpenSSF Model-Signing + in-toto ITE-6/SLSA for supply-chain interop.
alreadyFrontier: Per-tool-call Ed25519 signed receipt binding {tool, args_hash, result_hash, tenant, ts, transport, server_id} - matches/exceeds Signet & KYA/Vouched (src/mcp-gateway.js wrapToolCall/signMcpReceipt)
Externally-verifiable, deterministic, no-network verify with canonical-JSON over a frozen signed-field set and signature-self-exclusion (verifyMcpReceipt L278, canonicalForSigning L223)
Tamper-evidence proven by tests on args/result/tenant/tool/is_error/ts and key-swap-with-stale-fingerprint (wave921-mcp-signed-receipts #3-#6)
Result-passthrough contract: gateway returns unmodified bytes and binds them by hash - the 'no middle layer altered the output' guarantee the research calls for
Strict tenant fence on dispatch+verify, never trusting body (mcp-gateway-routes _tenantIdOf L45, defense-in-depth L220)

Have the rare frontier piece the research says almost no one combines: Ed25519 + RFC6962/9162 Merkle batch anchoring with offline inclusion proofs + signed checkpoints (transparency-anchor.js) - and in-toto/SLSA + OpenSSF Model-Signing export (intoto-receipt.js)
Two-tier prompt-injection guardrail with OWASP-category decision ladder and an out-of-process transformer seam (gateway-guardrail.js)

gaps.gap: MCP receipts are NOT anchored to the Merkle transparency log and cannot be exported as in-toto/SLSA - transparency-anchor.js and intoto-receipt.js only understand kolm-audit-1 receipts (intoto buildInferencePredicate copies receipt_id/namespace_id/route_decision, not call_id/tool/args_hash). So the 'prove the tool call EXISTED at time T' + supply-chain-tooling interop - the exact frontier differentiator the research highlights - stops at inference receipts and never reaches MCP tool calls.

In production the MCP gateway is registered with only {authMiddleware} (router L28624): no persistence store, so receipts live in a process-local Map (mcp-gateway-routes L97) and GET /v1/mcp/verify/:id 404s after any restart/redeploy; and no `execute`, so the gateway cannot actually proxy/invoke an upstream MCP server - it only records receipts for caller-supplied precomputed results. The 'governed proxy that intercepts every tool call' promise is not realized end-to-end in prod.

No tool-poisoning / injection-via-tool-output defense on the MCP path: applyGuardrail is wired into /v1/gateway/dispatch but NOT into /v1/mcp/dispatch or wrapToolCall. Tool args and (per CyberArk) tool OUTPUTS are never screened, despite the research naming TPA/output-poisoning the #1 MCP risk.

No tool-metadata pinning / rug-pull detection: nothing hashes or signs the tool name+description+inputSchema at registration so a later silent swap (rug pull) is invisible. The receipt binds args/result but not the *advertised tool contract*. Registration-workflow signing of tool metadata is an explicit frontier mitigation (Solo.io/Posta).
intoto-receipt-routes also registered with only {authMiddleware} (router L28623) - no getReceipt/store seam wired - so the in-toto attestation endpoint cannot resolve a stored receipt in prod.

No per-tool authorization / ACL or policy hook on dispatch (Kong/Permit-style per-tool allow/deny). Auth is tenant-level only; any authenticated tenant can mint a receipt for any tool name with no policy gate.
Receipt does not bind caller/agent identity, MCP session id, or upstream server-response provenance (only an optional opaque server_id string) - weaker than KYA's per-actor trail.
